item.title: this i believe
item.description: I care about … face-to-face communication open source development weak link strategies

item.title: **Cup of Joe
item.description: – in progress – A podcast for and by students fueled by intellectual excitement.

item.title: *JAR v1
item.description: – in progress – Autonomous rover, traverses College campus, recognizing sidewalk conditions (rain, snow, sleet, etc.) and responding accordingly.

item.title: chirc
item.description: implemented fully-functional IRC (Internet Relay Chat) Server, usinc C. Class: CMSC 23300 – Networks & Distributed Systems Description: Your implementation must be compliant enough with the official IRC specification for other IRC clients (not programmed by you) to work with your server. Although we will provide some scaffolding, most tasks will require you to consult the official IRC specification, or to experiment with existing IRC servers. Thus, this project will allow you to develop not just your network programming skills, but also your ability to read and interpret a real network protocol.

item.title: GARY 1.0
item.description: ideated & implemented prototype data viz web app for student data, currently in use at educational non-profit in South Side Chicago. Partner Organization: Gary Comer Youth Center GARY 1.0 can be found here

item.title: shell
item.description: created basic shell, using C. Class: CMSC 15400 – Introduction to Computer Systems Objectives: To write a complete program from scratch. We only provide a small code skeleton. To learn about shell functionalities. You will familiarize yourself with the Linux programming environment and gain exposure to the necessary functionality in shells. To learn about process interaction. You will learn about how a process is started, how a parent process waits for the child to terminate, how processes communicate via pipes, and so on.

item.title: No Photo, No Vote-o
item.description: Get full text of chapel talk.

Hi, I’m Joe! I’m a studen podcast recommendations The Ezra Klein Show Anil Dash on the biases of tech Bryan Stevenson Peter Beinart on the conscience of a liberal, American Jew Jaron Lanier’s case for deleting social media right now (I did!) This American Life Rom-Com Harper High School How I Built This Slack & Flickr: Stewart Butterfield Lyft: John Zimmer Instagram: Kevin Systrom & Mike Krieger Slow Burn, Season 1 Every episode.

item.title: about me
item.description: Joe Berusch basic facts attends university of chicago ‘21 studies computer science from Cleveland Heights & Shaker Heights, Ohio topics that fascinate & excite me serious startups computer networks && architecture ML && AI && the singularity urban design & housing segregation juvenile sentencing policy less serious musical theater tech history spikeball on the beach live recordings of rock performances (Springsteen, Eagles, Queen) meeting new people podcasts i listened to
